title: Other drivers and transport operatives n.e.c.
description: Plan, direct, or coordinate transportation, storage, or distribution activities in accordance with organizational policies and applicable government laws or regulations. Includes logistics managers. Direct or coordinate production, purchasing, warehousing, distribution, or financial forecasting services or activities to limit costs and improve accuracy, customer service, or safety. Examine existing procedures or opportunities for streamlining activities to meet product distribution needs. Direct the movement, storage, or processing of inventory. Operate, install, adjust, and maintain integrated computer/communications systems, consoles, simulators, and other data acquisition, test, and measurement instruments and equipment, which are used to launch, track, position, and evaluate air and space vehicles. May record and interpret test data. (Based on 57 related US occupations.)

Impact of Decisions on Co-workers or Company Results

tasks.task: Load baggage or cargo for crew or passengers. Refuel aircraft using hoses connected to fuel trucks. Guide aircraft to designated areas using hand signals, batons, or other methods. Tow aircraft to gates or hangars using tugs, tractors, or other vehicles. Empty aircraft lavatory systems or refill them with sanitizer fluid. Change aircraft oil, coolant, or other fluids. Inspect aircraft components to locate cracks, breaks, leaks, or other problems. Clean aircraft interiors by picking up waste, wiping down windows, or vacuuming. Polish aircraft exteriors. Wash the aircraft exteriors using lifts, cranes, detergent, or other equipment. Remove exhaust stains from aircraft using cleaning fluids. Refill aircraft potable water tanks. Apply de-icing fluid to aircraft from baskets lifted by truck-mounted cranes. Climb ladders to reach aircraft surfaces to be cleaned. De-grease aircraft exteriors. Record data in ships' logs, such as weather conditions or distances traveled. Radio to flight dispatchers or other personnel to discuss incoming or outgoing aircraft. Record orders for changes in ship speed or direction, and note gauge readings or test data, such as revolutions per minute or voltage output, in engineering logs or bellbooks. Maintain government-issued certifications, as required.

Overhaul lifeboats or lifeboat gear and lower or raise lifeboats with winches or falls. Lower and man lifeboats when emergencies occur. Monitor availability, use, or condition of lifesaving equipment or pollution preventatives to ensure that international regulations are followed. Conduct pre-operational checks on equipment to ensure proper functioning. Perform or participate in emergency drills, as required. Maintain desired courses, using compasses or electronic navigational aids. Follow safety procedures to ensure the protection of passengers, cargo, and vessels. Control traffic and maintain government-issued certifications, as required. Supervise marine engine technicians engaged in the maintenance or repair of mechanical or electrical marine vessels, and inspect their work to ensure that it is performed properly. Maintain complete records of engineering department activities, including machine operations. Maintain a ship's engines under the direction of the ship's engineering officers. Report any observed navigational hazards to authorities. Direct safety operations in emergency situations. Issue directions for loading, unloading, and seating in boats. Secure boats to docks with mooring lines, and cast off lines to enable departure. Clean engine parts and keep engine rooms clean. Pump water to clear machinery pipelines. Arrange repairs, fuel, and supplies for vessels. Organize and direct the activities of crew members.

Load or unload materials, vehicles, or passengers from vessels. Drive and maneuver equipment equipped with blades in successive passes over working areas to remove topsoil, vegetation, or rocks or to distribute and level earth or terrain. Operate, maintain, or repair ship equipment, such as winches, cranes, derricks, or weapons systems. Maintain or repair engines, electric motors, pumps, winches, or other mechanical or electrical equipment, or assist other crew members with maintenance or repair duties. Clean, lubricate, and refill equipment. Monitor and test operations of engines or other equipment so that malfunctions and their causes can be identified. Examine machinery to verify specified pressures or lubricant flows. Adjust handwheels and depress pedals to control attachments, such as blades, buckets, scrapers, or swing booms. Start power winches that draw in or let out cables to change positions of dredges, or pull in and let out cables manually. Maintain electrical power, heating, ventilation, refrigeration, water, or sewerage systems.

Visit sites to check fuel supplies to ensure adequate availability. Monitor operations to ensure that health and safety standards are met. Learn and follow safety regulations. Take actions to avoid potential hazards or obstructions, such as utility lines, other equipment, other workers, or falling objects. Drive machines onto truck trailers, and drive trucks to transport machines and material to and from job sites. Start engines to propel ships, and regulate engines and power transmissions to control speeds of ships, according to directions from captains or bridge computers. Document and prepare reports of accidents, unscheduled stops, or delays. Relay specified signals to other ships, using visual signaling devices, such as blinker lights or semaphores. Notify management of any changes in passenger schedules.

Remove operational debris or snow from streets, roads, or parking lots. Drive tractor-trailer trucks to move equipment from site to site. Signal engineers to begin train runs, stop trains, or change speed, using telecommunications equipment or hand signals. Keep records of the contents and destination of each train car, and make sure that cars are added or removed at proper points on routes. Receive instructions from dispatchers regarding trains' routes, timetables, and cargoes. Confer with engineers regarding train routes, timetables, and cargoes, and to discuss alternative routes when there are rail defects or obstructions. Monitor engine, machinery, or equipment indicators when vessels are underway, and report abnormalities to appropriate shipboard staff.

Talk to clients and study instructions, plans, or diagrams to establish work requirements. Perform general labor duties such as repairing booms. Drive and operate curbing machines to extrude concrete or asphalt curbing. Set up forms and lay out guidelines for curbs, according to written specifications, using string, spray paint, and concrete or water mixes.
Complete forms describing tasks completed. Mix cleaning compounds or solutions.
Confirm routes and destination information for freight cars and verify accuracy of timekeeping instruments with engineers to ensure trains depart on time.

Supervise and coordinate crew activities to transport freight and passengers, and provide assistance to elderly, sick, or injured people during boarding and exiting, while addressing passengers' questions, requests, or complaints.

Direct and instruct workers engaged in yard activities, such as switching tracks, coupling and uncoupling cars, handling inbound and outbound traffic, and signal transportation operators to stop or proceed.
Inspect freight cars and vehicles or equipment for compliance with safety regulations and procedures, including examining loaded cargo and ensuring equipment maintenance to maintain safety standards.
Prepare daily work and run schedules for workers or crews according to customer requests, specifications, or needs, coordinating equipment and personnel for efficient operation.
Collect and maintain detailed records of customer requests, delivery information, and freight conditions, ensuring documentation accuracy for operational reporting.
Monitor personnel or equipment locations and utilization to coordinate service and schedules, and advise crews on techniques for safely stowing dangerous or heavy cargo.
Prepare or review specifications or orders for safety equipment and ensure compliance with health and safety standards.
Assist in emergency situations by planning emergency response drills and providing necessary equipment or training related to safety procedures.
Conduct investigations of incidents or violations, such as delays, accidents, and equipment failures, to determine causes, and prepare reports on actions taken.
Ensure compliance with licensing requirements by inspecting credentials, licenses, or permits, and recommend corrective actions when infractions or problems are found.
Educate the public about health issues or enforce health legislation to promote safety in transportation operations and prevent disease.
Drive trucks to deliver items such as food, medical supplies, or newspapers, while obeying traffic laws and established transportation procedures.
Inspect vehicles or equipment for evidence of abuse, damage, or mechanical malfunction and ensure operational safety by monitoring machinery gauges and conducting safety checks.

Inspect and maintain vehicle supplies and equipment, such as gas, oil, water, tires, lights, and brakes, to ensure that vehicles are in proper working condition. Report any mechanical problems encountered with vehicles.

Present bills and receipts and collect payments for goods delivered or loaded. Maintain records, such as vehicle logs, records of cargo, or billing statements, in accordance with regulations. Prepare and submit reports that may include the number of passengers or trips, hours worked, mileage driven, fuel consumed, or fares received.

Load and unload trucks, vans, or automobiles. Verify the contents of inventory loads against shipping papers. Select loads or materials according to weight and size specifications. Check the condition of a vehicle's tires, brakes, windshield wipers, lights, oil, fuel, water, and safety equipment to ensure that everything is in working order.

Drive vehicles with capacities under three tons to transport materials to and from specified destinations, such as railroad stations, plants, residences, offices, or within industrial yards. Drive ambulances or assist ambulance drivers in transporting sick, injured, or convalescent persons. Provide passengers with assistance entering and exiting vehicles, and help them with any luggage.

Drive shuttle buses, limousines, company cars, or privately owned vehicles to transport passengers. Comply with traffic regulations to operate vehicles in a safe and courteous manner. Report delays, accidents, or other traffic and transportation situations, using telephones or mobile two-way radios. Communicate with dispatchers by radio, telephone, or computer to exchange information and receive requests for passenger service.
Respond to emergency situations by administering first aid, such as bandaging or splinting, and reporting facts concerning accidents or emergencies to appropriate personnel or officials.

Perform routine vehicle maintenance, such as regulating tire pressure and adding gasoline, oil, and water. Perform minor vehicle repairs, such as cleaning spark plugs, or take vehicles to mechanics for servicing. Maintain knowledge of first-aid procedures and earn and maintain appropriate certifications for operating vehicles like ambulances.

Report and record vehicle routes and inspect vehicle equipment, such as lights, brakes, horns, and windshield wipers, to ensure proper operation. Notify dispatchers or company mechanics of vehicle problems and perform emergency repairs, such as changing tires or installing light bulbs, as needed.

Follow relevant safety regulations and state laws governing vehicle operation, and ensure that passengers follow safety regulations. Secure passengers for transportation by buckling seatbelts or fastening wheelchairs with tie-down straps.

Document, analyze, and interpret test results or data for automotive systems, including recommendations for design improvements and system efficiencies. Test and evaluate performance, strength, and characteristics of vehicles and components, and conduct failure analyses to recommend corrective actions. Record numerical and graphical data, and prepare relevant specifications and documentation.

Drive heavy equipment and vehicles, perform preventative maintenance, and conduct manual activities such as clearing, grading, and landscaping work to ensure safety and efficiency in transportation operations. This includes operating machinery for snow clearing, road repairs, and debris removal from roadways.

Analyze energy requirements and efficiency of transportation systems, including testing vehicles that utilize alternative fuels or lighter materials, and studying design impacts on fuel efficiency. Participate in research related to intelligent transportation systems and automated vehicle applications.

Manage logistics and supply chain operations, including the planning and coordination of transportation, storage, distribution activities, and warehouse operations to optimize performance and reduce costs. This also involves supervising staff activities, ensuring compliance with policies and regulations, and documenting operational procedures.

Monitor and inspect equipment and machinery to ensure proper functioning and perform necessary repairs or calibrations to maintain operational integrity. This includes overseeing the testing of electromechanical assemblies and equipment used in transportation and logistics processes.

Establish inventory and documentation systems for tracking materials and products in logistics operations. Prepare materials for shipment and ensure compliance with environmental and safety standards in supply chain processes.

Prepare and manage departmental budgets, including forecasting material costs and analyzing the financial impact of logistics and transportation changes. Evaluate supplier performance and negotiate with vendors to ensure quality and efficiency in production or logistics operations.

Identify and select new suppliers, and integrate new technologies and processes to improve logistics operations and service delivery. Review and implement procedures aligned with changing regulations or organizational policies regarding transportation and supply chain management.

Prepare management recommendations, such as proposed fee and tariff increases or schedule changes; analyze expenditures and other financial information to develop plans, policies, or budgets for increasing profits or improving services; maintain metrics, reports, process documentation, customer service logs, or training or safety records; and review work throughout the work process and at completion to ensure that it has been performed properly.

Advise sales and billing departments of transportation charges for customers' accounts; examine invoices and shipping manifests for conformity to tariff and customs regulations; and verify that transportation and handling procedures meet regulatory requirements.

Confer with department heads to coordinate warehouse activities, such as production, sales, records control, or purchasing; participate in the coordination of engineering changes, product line extensions, or new product launches; and build or assemble robotic devices or systems.

Implement specific customer requirements, such as internal reporting or customized transportation metrics; and develop or implement plans for facility modification or expansion, such as equipment purchase or changes in space allocation or structural design.

Evaluate contractors or business partners for operational efficiency or safety or environmental performance records; negotiate with carriers, warehouse operators, or insurance company representatives for services and preferential rates; and check specifications of materials loaded or unloaded against information contained in work orders.

Train new employees in areas such as safety procedures or equipment operation; interview, select, and train warehouse and supervisory personnel; and evaluate employee performance and prepare performance appraisals.

Plan or implement energy saving changes to transportation services, such as reducing routes, optimizing capacities, employing alternate modes of transportation, or minimizing idling; and design or implement supply chains that support business strategies adapted to changing market conditions, new business opportunities, or cost reduction strategies.

Document physical supply chain processes, such as workflows, cycle times, position responsibilities, or system flows; and develop procedures for coordination of supply chain management with other functional areas, such as sales, marketing, finance, production, or quality assurance.

Select transportation routes to maximize economy by combining shipments or consolidating warehousing and distribution; and identify or investigate new opportunities for materials to be collected and recycled.
Record information, such as items received and delivered and recipients' responses to messages; and maintain logs of recycling materials received or shipped to processing companies.

Operate industrial trucks, tractors, loaders, and other equipment to transport materials to and from transportation vehicles and loading docks, and to store and retrieve materials in warehouses; and check conditions and weights of vessels to ensure cleanliness and compliance with loading procedures.
Plan work schedules and assign duties to maintain adequate staff for effective performance of activities and response to fluctuating workloads; and distribute cargo to maximize use of space.

Direct ground crews in the loading, unloading, securing, or staging of aircraft cargo or baggage, and provide training to recycling technicians or community service workers on topics such as safety, solid waste processing, or general recycling operations.
Perform the same work duties as those supervised, or perform more difficult or skilled tasks or assist in their performance.

Inspect food products and processing procedures to determine whether products are safe to eat; monitor the operations and sanitary conditions of slaughtering or meat processing plants; inspect the cleanliness and practices of establishment employees; examine, weigh, and measure commodities; and test samples for specific gravity.

Inspect or test horticultural products or livestock to detect harmful diseases, chemical residues, or infestations and to determine the quality of products or animals; and investigate violations of solid waste or recycling ordinances.

Fabricate housings, jigs, fittings, or fixtures, using metalworking machines; operate metalworking machines to fabricate housings, and assist engineers in the design, configuration, or application of robotic systems.
Develop, test, or program new robots; train others to install, use, or maintain robots; and analyze information about supplier performance or procurement program success.

Conduct or oversee the conduct of life cycle analyses to determine the environmental impacts of products, processes, or systems; and prepare and maintain work records and reports of information such as employee time and wages, daily receipts, or inspection results.

Participate in the hiring process by reviewing credentials, conducting interviews, or making hiring decisions or recommendations; and recommend or initiate personnel actions, such as promotions, transfers, or disciplinary measures.

Record operating data such as products and quantities pumped, gauge readings, and operating times, manually or using computers; and check with home offices after completed deliveries to confirm deliveries and collections and to receive instructions for other deliveries.

Organize recycling collection schedules to optimize service and efficiency; inspect physical condition of recycling or hazardous waste facility for compliance with safety, quality, and service standards; and implement grant-funded projects, monitoring and reporting progress in accordance with sponsoring agency requirements.

Review customer requests for service to determine service needs and deploy appropriate resources to provide service; and develop community or corporate recycling plans and goals to minimize waste and conform to resource constraints.
Quote prices to customers and provide information about facilities, entertainment options, and rules and regulations.
Schedule times of shipment and modes of transportation for materials, and plan work assignments and equipment allocations to meet transportation or production goals.

Assess training needs of staff and arrange or provide appropriate instruction, conduct employee training in equipment operations or work and safety procedures, and explain and demonstrate work tasks to new workers or assign training tasks to experienced workers.
Resolve personnel problems, complaints, or formal grievances when possible, and confer with management or subordinates to resolve worker problems, reviews, or grievances.
Inspect equipment for wear and for conformance to specifications, and maintain or verify records of time, materials, expenditures, or crew activities.
Counsel employees in work-related activities, personal growth, or career development, and evaluate employee performance.
Conduct staff meetings to relay general information or to address specific topics, such as safety, and keep informed of shut-down and emergency evacuation procedures.
Maintain inventories of equipment, storing and retrieving items, and maintaining or repairing worksites, and monitor inventory levels and order supplies as necessary.

Direct and coordinate the activities of employees engaged in the production or processing of goods, such as inspectors, machine setters, or fabricators, and supervise or direct the work of non-destructive testing trainees or staff.
Monitor activities to ensure adherence to rules and safety procedures, and enforce safety rules and regulations.
Move freight, stock, or other materials to and from storage or production areas, loading docks, delivery vehicles, ships, or containers, by hand or using trucks, tractors, or other equipment.
Inspect materials, products, or equipment to detect defects or malfunctions, and examine, measure, or weigh cargo or materials to determine specific handling requirements.

Collect and sort recyclable materials from curbside for delivery to designated facilities and clean recycling yard by sweeping, raking, picking up broken glass and loose paper debris, or moving barrels and bins.
Record details of attendance, sales, receipts, reservations, or repair activities, and prepare, compile, and submit reports on work activities, operations, production, or work-related accidents.
Arrange for the removal of unruly patrons, and direct patrons to rides, seats, or attractions.

Inspect or test materials, stock, vehicles, equipment, or facilities to ensure that they are safe, free of defects, and consistent with specifications, and determine standards, budgets, production goals, and rates based on company policies, equipment and labor availability, and workloads.
Drive vehicles or operate machines or equipment to complete work assignments or to assist workers, and dispatch personnel and vehicles in response to telephone or radio reports of emergencies.
Direct or coordinate the activities of workers, train them in proper operational procedures, and explain company policies.
Compute or estimate cash, payroll, transportation, or personnel requirements; analyze and record personnel or operational data and write related activity reports.
Participate in continuing education to stay abreast of industry trends and developments.
Take disciplinary action to address performance problems and resolve customer complaints regarding worker performance or services rendered.
Requisition necessary supplies, equipment, or services; recruit and hire staff members.

Recommend and implement measures to improve worker motivation, work methods, or customer services; confer with customers, supervisors, contractors, or other personnel to exchange information or to resolve problems.

Observe and evaluate workers' appearance and performance to ensure quality service and compliance with specifications; inspect work areas or operating equipment to ensure conformance to established standards in areas such as cleanliness or maintenance.
Inspect materials, stock, vehicles, equipment, or facilities to ensure that they are safe, free of defects, and consistent with specifications; enforce safety rules and regulations.
Inform workers about interests or special needs of specific groups; explain and demonstrate work tasks to new workers or assign training tasks to experienced workers.
Meet with managers or other supervisors to stay informed of changes affecting operations.
